cards.front: What does Insertion Sort use during the DECREASE-AND-CONQUER process?
cards.back: the elements in the already-sorted portion of the array

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Insertion Sort?
cards.back: O(n^2) in the worst case, but O(n) in the best case when the input is already sorted

cards.front: What is the space complexity of Insertion Sort?
cards.back: O(1) since it only requires a constant amount of additional space

cards.front: How does Insertion Sort compare to Bubble Sort?
cards.back: Insertion Sort is generally more efficient than Bubble Sort, especially for large datasets, because it only requires a single pass through the array

cards.front: What is the name of the algorithm used in the DECREASE-AND-CONQUER process in Insertion Sort?
